summary:
I've fixed the issue in the code generation engine. The problem was in the `codegen.rs` file where the getter methods for reference types were returning an owned value instead of a reference. I edited the code to return a reference to the nested struct by changing the format string in the `generate_getter` function.

Running the test script `./test.sh` now shows that all tests have passed, including the complex nested accessor tests which were previously failing. This confirms that the fix was successful and the needle in the haystack has been found and resolved.
